title: hieracium lycopifolium subsp. vallisiacum
intro: Hieracium lycopifolium subsp. vallisiacum, also known as Vallisi's hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its yellow or orange flowers and edible leaves. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as dyspepsia, and skin conditions, like eczema, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in vitamins A, C, and potassium, offering several health benefits, including antioxidant and antiseptic properties that can help soothe digestive issues and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Vallisi's hawkweed can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often combined with other herbs to create remedies for various health conditions.
